headline: Lazy-resolve PROJECT in hook_init — ~9ms per hook in real-session

bullets: lib/hook-utils.sh: hook_init no longer resolves PROJECT eagerly. New _ensure_project accessor resolves on first read and caches; logging functions and lessons-aware hooks call it before reading $PROJECT.
Hooks that never reference $PROJECT skip the resolution entirely. N=50 probe: real-session hook_init p50 19.0ms → 9.6ms (−9.4ms), p95 25.2ms → 12.5ms.

Two-thirds of the saving is the sqlite3 fork in _resolve_project_id; the rest is the subshell that was capturing the function output. Both costs were paid on every hook invocation, regardless of whether $PROJECT was used.
